text: This PR addresses 46 performance audit findings across 30 frontend component files. Most changes are correct and well-executed: dynamic imports, useMemo for derived data, targeted watch() calls, stable component references, and GPU-composited CSS animations. However, 2 critical regressions block merge — a security bypass in ProtectedRoute (String.includes instead of Array.includes for role matching) and an invisible progress bar in TierPathVisualizer (missing right-0). Additionally, 5 landing page components have broken scroll animations from transition-all→transition-opacity without transition-transform.

reasoning: Two critical issues block merge: (1) ProtectedRoute.tsx:24 uses String.includes() for role matching — ORGANIZATION_ADMIN users can bypass ADMIN-only route restrictions via substring match; (2) TierPathVisualizer.tsx:70 absolutely-positioned progress bar lacks right-0, making scaleX(0 → 100%) produce 0px width — bar is invisible. Four landing pages have broken scroll animations (missing transition-transform). All fixes are straightforward.

message: Correctly replaced blanket watch() with 8 targeted watch() calls for individual fields. Prevents unnecessary re-renders on unrelated field changes and provides precise useMemo dependencies.
Extracted currentTier computation outside the map loop. Eliminates O(n²) lookup pattern (levels.find inside levels.map) to O(n). Correct and effective.
Added useMemo for chartData derivation from data prop. Prevents recharts from re-rendering all cells on every parent render. Deps [data] are correct.

Extracted ThemeToggleButton to module scope with useCallback and imported from components/theme/ThemeToggle. Fixes remount-on-every-render bug where inline component definition created new type identity each render.
Replaced conditional mount ({mobileOpen && ...}) with Tailwind hidden class. Mobile menu DOM is now preserved across toggles — avoids unnecessary component remount and state loss.
Added loading="lazy" to Image component. Correctly defers loading for badges below the fold in grid views. Also removed inline IIFE pattern for icon rendering.
Added next/dynamic import for EventSeriesForm. Prevents ~440 lines of form logic from being bundled when showRecurringOption is false.
Added next/dynamic import for RichTextEditor. Prevents @tiptap bundle (~80-100KB) from being included in initial JS payload.
Replaced namespace import import * as Sentry with named import import { captureException } from '@sentry/nextjs'. Enables tree-shaking. Also fixed relative import to @/ alias.
Added duplicate guard (!certifications.includes(trimmed)) to addCertification. Prevents duplicate cert entries that were previously possible.
Combined separate type import and value import from @/lib/shared into a single statement.
Wrapped toggle function in useCallback with [setTheme, resolvedTheme] deps. Stabilizes event handler reference for this simple button component.
Reduced blur-3xl to blur-xl and added will-change-transform. Mitigates expensive GPU compositing from large blur radii on decorative backgrounds.
Added index suffix to stat keys. Prevents potential React key collisions if two stat cards had identical labels (e.g. two 'Active' cards).

rolesKey.includes(user.role) uses String.prototype.includes() which does SUBSTRING matching. Since role values include both 'ADMIN' and 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN', the string 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN,SUPER_ADMIN'.includes('ADMIN') returns true, allowing ORGANIZATION_ADMIN users to bypass ADMIN-only route restrictions. This is a security regression introduced by the autofix.
suggestion: Keep rolesKey in the dependency array for stability, but use allowedRoles.includes(user.role) for the actual check: const rolesKey = allowedRoles.join(','); ... if (!allowedRoles.includes(user.role)) { ... }. The eslint exhaustive-deps rule can be disabled for this line.

message: Absolutely-positioned progress fill div has left:0 but no right:0 or explicit width. Since the empty div defaults to width: 0, transform: scaleX(completionPct/100) × 0px = 0px — the progress bar is invisible regardless of completionPct. Regression when width-based styling was replaced with transform.
suggestion: Add right-0 to the className string so the div spans the full container width before scaleX reduces it: className="absolute top-1/2 left-0 right-0 h-1..."

message: Changed from motion-safe:transition-all to motion-safe:transition-opacity but the scroll animation uses both opacity and translate-y. Without transition-transform, the vertical slide snaps instantly instead of animating smoothly.
suggestion: Add motion-safe:transition-transform alongside motion-safe:transition-opacity: motion-safe:transition-opacity motion-safe:transition-transform motion-safe:duration-700

message: Same transition-opacity-only issue as CTA.tsx. The translate-y scroll animation will not animate.
Same transition-opacity-only issue as CTA.tsx and ImpactStats.tsx. Translate-y animation will snap.
Same transition-opacity-only issue. The translate-y scroll animation will not animate smoothly.

VolunteerRow is wrapped in memo() (line 40) but all callback props — onToggle (line 388), onHoursChange (line 392), onRatingChange (line 395), onApprove (line 399) — are inline arrow functions created as new references every render. The memo() shallow comparison always detects prop changes, preventing any skip of re-render.
suggestion: Wrap callbacks in useCallback: const handleToggle = useCallback((id) => ... , [volunteers]); or pass a dispatch-style handler. Consider passing volunteerId instead of the full volunteer object for stable comparisons.

message: The sizes prop value (max-width: 1024px) 50vw overestimates actual image width. The md:grid-cols-3 grid renders each image at ~33vw, causing Next.js to select a larger-than-needed image.
suggestion: Change to (max-width: 768px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 33vw, 33vw for more precise responsive image sizing.

message: const idx on line 102 shadows the outer idx parameter from certifications.map((cert, idx) => ...) on line 94. While functionally correct (indexOf returns the correct index), this is a lint violation and reduces code clarity.
suggestion: Rename to const removeIndex = certifications.indexOf(cert) and use filter((_, j) => j !== removeIndex).
